Overall: No data were found regarding the efficacy of any drinking water treatment process for the control of tebufenozide.  Physical/chemical property data are relatively scant. Its Henry's law constant suggests it is not likely air strippable.  Its Koc suggests it may be bound to soil particles and be controlled during coagulation or precipitative softening.  Using Polanyi models, its molecular weight, solubilty and density suggest it would be strongly adsorbed to granular activated carbon or controlled by powdered activated carbon.  Its structure suggests it may be reactive to stronger oxidants like chlorine, chlorine dioxide, ozone or advanced oxidation processes utilizing the hydroxyl readical, resulting in transformation products.  Its size suggests it would be controlled by reverse osmosis.
